subtasks.description: Orchestrate multi-tool workflow: retrieve project requirements from Jira API, check team availability via calendar API, schedule standup meeting in calendar system, and post agenda to Slack. This requires coordinating 4 distinct enterprise tools from the registry and sequencing them based on dependencies (requirements → availability check → meeting scheduling → agenda posting).

plan.cost_rationale: This query explicitly asks for multi-tool orchestration across 4 distinct enterprise tools (Jira, Calendar API, meeting scheduler, Slack) with inter-step dependencies and sequencing logic. This is the textbook use case for multitool_plan specialist. A single frontier-tier subtask is assigned because: (1) the user asks for one unified orchestration workflow, not independent queries; (2) frontier tier is necessary to handle the complexity of tool registry lookup, dependency sequencing, conditional branching (e.g., if availability gaps exist), and data transformation between systems; (3) lower tiers cannot reliably generate correct multi-tool invocation plans with proper error handling and state transitions. One subtask is sufficient because the entire orchestration is a single cohesive workflow, not separate analytical problems.

arguments.title: Team Standup Meeting
arguments.description: Daily standup to discuss project requirements and progress

arguments.message:
📋 **Team Standup Agenda - Daily Sync**
